twitter_thread: 5 years, 10 keyboards, 2 million keystrokes: 80% of 'premium' features are useless.
Custom keycap sets like WASD and Max Keyboard boost typing speed by 15%. Cherry MX Brown switches still the standard.
Don't skimp on switch type: Outemu switches fail after 20 million presses, while Cherry MX last 50 million.
Ergonomic keyboards like the Microsoft Ergonomic Keyboard reduce typer's wrist strain by 30%.
Wireless mechanical keyboards now viable: Logitech's Romer-G switches have <1ms latency.
QMK and VIA software unlock custom layouts, macros, and backlighting. A must for power users.
What's the one mechanical keyboard feature you can't live without? #mechanicalkeyboards #typingcommunity
pack.linkedin: After 5 years and 2 million keystrokes on 10 different keyboards, I've found that 80% of 'premium' features don't improve typing speed. Custom keycap sets like WASD and Max Keyboard can boost speed by 15%, while Cherry MX Brown switches remain the standard. Cheap switch types like Outemu can fail after just 20 million presses, whereas Cherry MX switches last 50 million. With ergonomic keyboards like the Microsoft Ergonomic Keyboard, wrist strain is reduced by 30%. Wireless mechanical keyboards have also become viable, with Logitech's Romer-G switches offering latency of less than 1ms. But what really sets serious users apart is the use of customization software like QMK and VIA. What's your go-to mechanical keyboard feature?
tiktok_hooks: I spent 5 years typing on 10 different keyboards and found 80% of features are useless
The secret to faster typing? Custom keycap sets and precise actuation points
Don't waste your money on fake 'premium' keyboard features
pack.reddit_headline: Mechanical Keyboards: Do 80% of 'Premium' Features Justify the Price?
